entities.name: WATER HYACINTH
entities.description: An aquatic plant used as biomass for biochar production

entities.name: BIOCHAR
entities.description: A carbon-rich material produced from biomass via pyrolysis used for soil amendment

entities.name: MAIZE
entities.description: The test crop used to evaluate the impact of biochar

entities.name: PYROLYSIS TEMPERATURE
entities.description: The temperature at which biomass is converted to biochar, affecting its properties

entities.name: APPLICATION RATE
entities.description: The amount of biochar applied to the soil

entities.name: POT EXPERIMENT
entities.description: The experimental setup used to test the effects of biochar on maize and soil

entities.name: SOIL AMENDMENT
entities.description: The process of adding materials to soil to improve its properties

entities.name: SHOOT DRY BIOMASS
entities.description: A measure of maize growth above ground

entities.name: ROOT DRY BIOMASS
entities.description: A measure of maize growth below ground

entities.name: SOIL TOTAL NITROGEN
entities.description: A soil nutrient property improved by biochar application

entities.name: CATION EXCHANGE CAPACITY
entities.description: A soil property indicating the ability to hold exchangeable cations

entities.name: AMMONIUM-NITROGEN
entities.description: A form of nitrogen in the soil improved by biochar

entities.name: SOIL CARBON
entities.description: The amount of carbon stored in the soil

entities.name: SOIL PH
entities.description: The acidity or alkalinity of the soil

entities.name: ELECTRICAL CONDUCTIVITY
entities.description: A measure of the salt concentration in the soil

entities.name: AVAILABLE PHOSPHORUS
entities.description: The amount of phosphorus available for plant uptake
